comments_critique.summary: The discussion centers on the utility and reliability of HTTP versus HTTPS. One comment critiques the content quality of a website about HTTP, while another argues that HTTP remains valuable alongside HTTPS due to its simplicity and lower fragility.

comments.author: superkuh
comments.text:
HTTP is incomparibly less fragile than HTTPS which is why HTTP+HTTPS is such a great solution for websites made by human persons for human persons. Lets be clear, corporate or institutional persons using HTTPS alone is fine and reasonable. But for human use cases HTTP+HTTPS gets you the best of both worlds. No HTTPS cert system ever survives longer than a few years without human input/maintainence. There's just too much changing and too much complexity. From the software of the user to the software of the webserver.

Which is to say, HTTP is not some "ancient" tech like an analog television. It is a modern technology used today doing things that HTTPS can't.
comments.analysis: The comment provides a nuanced perspective on the complementary roles of HTTP and HTTPS, emphasizing maintenance challenges with HTTPS certificates and the practical benefits of HTTP's simplicity.

comments.author: paulnpace
comments.text: Not very useful when most of the pages are default web server pages.
comments.analysis: This comment critiques the website's content quality but does not engage deeply with the technical discussion about HTTP and HTTPS, making it less informative.
